title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
Detective Hakast and Colin Lamb interview Mrs. Hemm regarding her whereabouts during the afternoon of the murder. Mrs. Hemm states she was at home between 1:30 and 3:30 PM, grooming her pets and attempting to lure her kitten, Arabella, down from a tree. Hakast requests to see her backyard, which is overgrown with thick shrubs and laurels. Mrs. Hemm mentions that Mrs. Lyme’s two sons, whom she describes as "hoodlums" with air rifles, live in the south end of the crescent in gardens adjacent to hers. Hakast observes that the dense vegetation makes the gardens feel isolated, noting that the victim did not live in the house at number 19, where a blind woman resides, but rather came there to be murdered.

The investigators then travel to number 61, a property located behind Mrs. Hemm's house that is connected to number 19 at one corner. They meet Mr. Jotham Brand, a friendly, wealthy man living in a luxuriously furnished home. Brand discusses local rumors regarding the murder, noting conflicting reports about whether the victim was strangled, stabbed, or beaten. Hakast reveals that the victim left a business card in his pocket and shows Brand a police photo of the deceased. Brand claims he does not recognize the man and has not had any visitors for sales purposes. When Hakast suggests questioning Brand's wife, Valerie, Brand hesitates, noting she is unwell. Brand remarks that the photo of the murdered man does not look unpleasant, stating the victim looks as if he is merely sleeping.
